events.title: Severe sandstorm sweeps through Gaza tent camps
events.description: Sandstorms swept through tent camps in southern and central Gaza, tearing down shelters and burying belongings under sand as thousands of displaced Palestinians struggled to hold onto their makeshift homes. Families in areas like Khan Younis and Gaza City say powerful winds repeatedly collapsed tents, worsening already dire living conditions after months of conflict. Meanwhile, violence has continued despite a ceasefire, with casualties reported in recent weeks.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Severe sandstorm sweeps through Gaza tent camps

events.title: The humanitarian crisis worsens in Lebanon, with over 850,000 displaced
events.description: In just 10 days, more than 800,000 people in Lebanon have been displaced by war, just over a year since the last conflict uprooted over a million Lebanese from their homes. Israeli strikes have killed 826 people, including 106 children and 65 women, since the conflict between Hezbollah and Israel reignited on March 2, according to the Health Ministry. Details by Kevin Charbel, head of Première Urgence Internationale mission in Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The humanitarian crisis worsens in Lebanon, with over 850,000 displaced

events.title: Myanmar at a ‘crossroads’: The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert
events.description: More than five years after Myanmar’s military coup, international resolve to hold the junta accountable must not weaken, an independent human rights expert warned on Friday, as escalating violence and growing humanitarian needs push millions of civilians deeper into crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Myanmar at a ‘crossroads’: The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert

events.title: Civil war in Sudan is a never-ending humanitarian crisis
events.description: Almost three years into Sudan's war, drone attacks and sexual violence show no signs of relenting. Could intensified international efforts influence the situation?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Civil war in Sudan is a never-ending humanitarian crisis

events.title: India news: Army says 'Pakistani terrorist' killed in Kashmir
events.description: The Indian army said the slain militant, who was killed after he opened fire, was found with "warlike stores." Meanwhile, the government has released new rules to manage the LPG shortage.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India news: Army says 'Pakistani terrorist' killed in Kashmir

events.title: War-driven energy price spikes highlight value of renewables: UN climate chief
events.description: The disruption of global energy supplies is being felt worldwide, the UN’s top climate change official warned on Monday, as conflict in the Middle East drives oil and gas prices sharply higher – echoing the market turmoil triggered by the war in Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War-driven energy price spikes highlight value of renewables: UN climate chief

events.title: Asia scrambles to confront energy crisis unleashed by Iran war – with no end in sight
events.description: From fuel caps to four-day work weeks, the Middle East conflict has left the world’s top crude oil importing region desperate to shore up supplies Donald Trump has scrambled in recent days to reassure the world that the economic impact of his war on Iran can be contained. Sure, one of the most important waterways in global trade has, in effect, been shut for almost two weeks – but it might reopen before long. In the meantime, US oil-related sanctions on “some countries” will be lifted. And besides, the entire conflict could be over soon . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Asia scrambles to confront energy crisis unleashed by Iran war – with no end in sight

events.title: Are Iran's retaliatory strikes across the Gulf region affecting the US' war strategy?
events.description: Israel's military said it began a broad wave of strikes on western Iran on Sunday. As the war progresses into the third week, further retaliatory strikes from Iran are likely to occur. So far, Iran has launched strikes across nine countries in the Gulf region. Most of these attacks have been intercepted but at least 19 people have been killed. The US and Israel started this war but it seems the Gulf states are bearing the brunt of Iran's retaliation. Could this affect the United States' strategy in the war? Analysis by Joe Macaron, geopolitical analyst and non-resident association researcher at the Arab World Institute in Paris.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Are Iran's retaliatory strikes across the Gulf region affecting the US' war strategy?

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 12 March: Shipping attacks, rising oil prices and widening humanitarian crisis
events.description: War continues across the Middle East, with impacts growing far beyond the region. Oil prices are hovering around $100 a barrel as attacks on shipping and energy infrastructure disrupt key supply routes. Strikes and counterstrikes between Israel, Hezbollah and Iran continue, while ships have reportedly been hit in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman, sending shocks through the global economy. Meanwhile, UN agencies are expanding humanitarian relief efforts as displacement rises and civilian needs grow. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 12 March: Shipping attacks, rising oil prices and widening humanitarian crisis

events.title: Israel hits back after coordinated Iran-Hezbollah missile, drone strikes, urges Beirut to rein in terrorists
events.description: Hezbollah fired 200 missiles and drones into Israel in what Israeli media called an integrated attack with Iran, prompting fierce Israeli Defense Forces strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel hits back after coordinated Iran-Hezbollah missile, drone strikes, urges Beirut to rein in terrorists

events.title: World News in Brief: Deadly drone attacks in Sudan, violence against women in the spotlight, more clashes in DR Congo
events.description: UN human rights chief Volker Türk said on Thursday he was appalled by the devastating impact on civilians of increasing drone attacks in Sudan, amid reports that more than 200 civilians have been killed by drones since 4 March alone, in the Kordofan region and White Nile state.

events.title: How the conflict in Iran is affecting global markets
events.description: Escalation of the Iran conflict is sending shockwaves through global markets — driving up oil, fuel, and commodity prices, stoking inflation and recession risks worldwide. We hear from three NPR reporters in Europe, Asia and Russia.

events.title: Trump administration threatens media outlets over Iran war coverage
events.description: The head of the Federal Communications Commission, which oversees US media outlets, has warned that some broadcasters risk losing their licenses over their news coverage of the Iran war. Brendan Carr – a Trump appointee – wrote in a social media post that broadcasters must operate in the public interest and those that are running hoaxes and fake news have the chance now to correct course before their license renewals come up. Free speech groups blasted Carr's warning, calling it outrageous. Analysis by Philip Turle, international affairs editor.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump administration threatens media outlets over Iran war coverage

events.title: Pope Leo urges war leaders to halt fighting after deadly strike on school sparks outrage
events.description: Pope Leo XIV called Sunday for an immediate ceasefire in the war involving Iran, urgently urging world leaders to halt violence after deadly strikes hit schools and civilian areas.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo urges war leaders to halt fighting after deadly strike on school sparks outrage

events.title: Trump seeks warships from other countries to help secure Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Trump calls for international warships to secure Strait of Hormuz as Iran allegedly threatens global oil shipping routes from the Middle East region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump seeks warships from other countries to help secure Strait of Hormuz

events.title: UK government axes flagship global health project
events.description: Programme which supports schemes in six African countries was previously hailed as vital protection for Britain against future pandemics A flagship health project in Africa, which UK ministers said would play a vital role in protecting Britain from future pandemic threats, is being axed due to aid cuts, the Guardian can reveal. The Global Health Workforce Programme (GHWP) which supported development and training for healthcare staff in six African countries, will close at the end of the month, the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) said. Continue reading...

events.title: BoM forecasts high chance of cyclone forming in Coral Sea – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Japan and Australia say they have no plans to send ships to strait of Hormuz as Trump increases pressure Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Discovery of fire ant nest at New Farm park reflects growing need to deal with threat, group says Last week, the Invasive Species Council said fire ant nests had been detected and destroyed in World Heritage rainforest in the Gold Coast hinterland . The ants have also been detected on sporting fields across Brisbane and surrounds, including at Mount Gravatt, Rocklea, Coorparoo and the Redlands. That’s an iconic location in Brisbane. If we don’t eradicate fire ants, this is going to happen everywhere in the country. Brisbane is getting a taste of it now. Local sporting clubs need to be alert to this. For the time being it’s an increased maintenance burden for them. An insecticide which instantly kills fire ants was injected directly into the nest. The affected area was temporarily closed while treatment was under way but has since reopened and is safe to use. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ BoM forecasts high chance of cyclone forming in Coral Sea – as it happened

events.title: In Beirut, Guterres demands end to Middle East war: Civilians ‘deserve to live without fear’
events.description: The UN Secretary-General on Saturday called on the international community to intensify support for the Government and people of Lebanon, warning that the south of the country “risks being turned into a wasteland.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 In Beirut, Guterres demands end to Middle East war: Civilians ‘deserve to live without fear’

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 11 March: Civilian toll rises, regional disruption spreads as Security Council meets
events.description: As the conflict in the Middle East continues to ripple across the region, the UN Security Council met today to discuss the escalating situation, with a morning briefing focused on Lebanon and a second session later on the wider crisis – adopting a Bahrain-led resolution, while a Russian-led text failed to secure enough votes. Across the UN system, agencies are warning of mounting humanitarian needs as displacement rises, services come under strain and regional instability disrupts mobility, trade and aid operations. Stay with us as we bring you the latest developments, reactions and updates from UN Headquarters and across the region. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 11 March: Civilian toll rises, regional disruption spreads as Security Council meets

events.title: Security Council adopts Gulf-backed resolution condemning Iran attacks; Russia bid fails
events.description: As war continues in the Middle East, the UN Security Council on Wednesday adopted a Bahrain-led resolution condemning Iranian attacks on several regional states, while a separate draft resolution introduced by Russia on the wider crisis failed to pass.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council adopts Gulf-backed resolution condemning Iran attacks; Russia bid fails

events.title: Trump urges allies to send warships to Strait of Hormuz
events.description: President Donald Trump has threatened to get the Strait of Hormuz opened "one way or another" and has called on a number of countries including France, the United Kingdom and China to send ships to the area to overcome Iran's blockade on the vital trade artery. It comes as global oil supplies are skyrocketing and supply continues to be strangled with Iran blockading the strait and targeting the Gulf's energy infrastructure in retaliation to US strikes. Story by Shirli Sitbon and Eliza Herbert.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump urges allies to send warships to Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Middle East crisis: UN health agency releases emergency funds for Lebanon, Iraq, Syria
events.description: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) is releasing another $2 million in emergency funds to support health systems in Lebanon, Iraq and Syria as strikes against Iran by the US and Israeli continue amid counterstrikes across the Gulf and wider region by Tehran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East crisis: UN health agency releases emergency funds for Lebanon, Iraq, Syria

events.title: An environmental activist and her family escaped death threats in Honduras. ICE deported her husband anyway
events.description: Oscar, Ana and their children fled violence for safety in the US. Now Oscar, afraid and alone, is back in Honduras – ‘at the mercy of God and his will’ As soon as Oscar’s deportation flight landed at the La Lima airport in Honduras, he put on his baseball cap. On the airport shuttle toward the terminal, he pulled his cap even lower – trying to obscure his face at various police checkpoints. His parents picked him up in a car, and drove him to a lodging they had arranged for him – miles away from his family home. He has hardly stepped outside since. “Because I can’t trust anyone – not the authorities, not the government, not a police officer,” he said. He has visited his mother a handful of times since the US deported him three weeks ago, and only under the cover of night. “They will kill anyone here. There is death everywhere.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 An environmental activist and her family escaped death threats in Honduras. ICE deported her husband anyway

events.title: US and Israel carry out new strikes on western Iran
events.description: Israel says it's carrying out fresh strikes on western Iran - as the war enters its third week. Israeli officials say the bombing campaign is wide-scale and targeting regime infrastructure. This comes as the IDF says it has detected missiles fired from Iran. Details by Hoda Abdel-Hamid, journalist in Doha.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US and Israel carry out new strikes on western Iran

events.title: Five killed in Russian strikes on Ukraine as peace talks stall
events.description: At least five people have been killed in Russian attacks on Ukraine, with a further 18 wounded, officials have said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Five killed in Russian strikes on Ukraine as peace talks stall

events.title: ‘Perfect storm’: Lebanon crisis deepens as civilians bear the brunt
events.description: Lebanon is facing a “perfect storm of unpredictable challenges” as conflict, mass displacement and dwindling humanitarian resources converge, the UN’s Resident and Humanitarian Coordinator in Lebanon, Imran Riza, has warned.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘Perfect storm’: Lebanon crisis deepens as civilians bear the brunt

events.title: An ‘alarming’ two-thirds of children say cyberbullying has increased
events.description: Around two-thirds of children worldwide report an increase in cyberbullying, with one in two say they don’t know how to get the right support, according to a recent poll carried out by the UN’s top official who works to end violence against children.
events.ticker_label: 💥 An ‘alarming’ two-thirds of children say cyberbullying has increased

events.title: Woman accused of fatal hit-and-run that killed 8-year-old laughs during first court appearance
events.description: A Florida woman accused in a fatal hit-and-run was seen laughing during a court hearing as the judge read charges for an 8-year-old boy's death in the Winter Haven crash incident.

events.title: 'A new norm': BBC visits Doha market starting to fill up again two weeks into Iran war
events.description: The BBC's International News correspondent, Barbara Plett Usher, visits Doha's Souq Waqif market as strikes in the region continue.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'A new norm': BBC visits Doha market starting to fill up again two weeks into Iran war

events.title: Fire erupts at Dubai airport following drone attack
events.description: Footage shows fire near Dubai International Airport after a drone ignited a fuel tank, according to UAE authorities.

events.title: From missiles strikes to rocketing oil price: Guy Laron speaks to France 24 to analyse the economic consequences of Iran war
events.description: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Guy Laron, Senior lecturer in international relations, the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, about the economic consequences of the ongoing war in Iran, from rising oil prices to how the strategic Strait of Hormuz can be used as an economic weapon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From missiles strikes to rocketing oil price: Guy Laron speaks to France 24 to analyse the economic consequences of Iran war

events.title: Aid operations strained across Middle East: WFP seeks $200 million to sustain food assistance
events.description: Rising food prices, growing displacement and disruptions to global supply routes are placing millions of people across the Middle East at greater risk of hunger, according to the World Food Programme (WFP), which warns that the humanitarian system is struggling to keep pace with expanding needs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Aid operations strained across Middle East: WFP seeks $200 million to sustain food assistance

events.title: Protesters torch Communist Party HQ in Cuba; video appears to capture gunfire
events.description: Dramatic footage appears to show a protester on the ground after alleged gunfire as crowds attack the Communist Party building in Cuba, chanting "Freedom!" amid crisis.

events.title: Pennsylvania men accused of ISIS-inspired bomb attack on NYC protesters near mayor's mansion: timeline
events.description: Two Pennsylvania men accused of ISIS-inspired terror plot threw homemade bombs at NYC protesters. Both explosive devices failed to detonate during the incident.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Pennsylvania men accused of ISIS-inspired bomb attack on NYC protesters near mayor's mansion: timeline

events.title: 'We never asked for a ceasefire,' says Iran's foreign minister, as war keeps raging
events.description: Israel announced a barrage of new strikes on western Iran on Sunday, while Iran's foreign minister said the country has not asked for a ceasefire as President Trump had claimed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'We never asked for a ceasefire,' says Iran's foreign minister, as war keeps raging

events.title: World News in Brief: Türk’s South Sudan ceasefire call, Ukraine strikes, Gaza food alert, Afghan returnees
events.description: UN human rights chief Volker Türk on Tuesday condemned accounts of “gruesome killings” of civilians in South Sudan, the destruction and poisoning of key water sources and fresh waves of mass displacement.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Türk’s South Sudan ceasefire call, Ukraine strikes, Gaza food alert, Afghan returnees

events.title: Mexico City sets the world record for the largest soccer class
events.description: Mexico City set a world record on Sunday with the largest soccer class ever, with 9,500 participants in the capital’s Zocalo square as the country prepares to host the opening match of the World Cup in June

events.title: Race on to establish globally recognised 'AI-free' logo
events.description: The backlash to the growing use of the tech has led to an explosion in attempts to come up with 'AI-Free' logo that could be used globally.

events.title: The kill line v Chinamaxxing: a window into how China and the US see each other
events.description: In China, one social media trend hangs on the idea that a life in the US is always one step from disaster, while another in the US has gen Z revelling in Chinese lifestyle hacks Across two online worlds that are normally splintered, over the last few months there has been a mirroring of sorts. On TikTok and Instagram, young people are diving into the joys of Chinese culture – from drinking hot water to playing mahjong – all under the banner of “Chinamaxxing”. On the Chinese internet, however, the US is losing its decades-long grip on soft power, and is instead being replaced by a darker trend: the kill line. The kill line is a dangerous place to be. In gaming, the term refers to the point at which a player’s strength is so depleted that one more blow could lead to total wipeout. In China, the term refers to the risks that come with daily life in the US. Continue reading...

events.title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds 15, with peace talks stalled
events.description: The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds 15, with peace talks stalled

events.title: Kosovo faces third legislative election in just over a year
events.description: Kosovo’s failure to elect a president has again plunged the nation into political deadlock. Now, it could face its third parliamentary election in just over a year. The country's international reputation is taking a hit.

events.title: Brother of Michigan synagogue attacker was Hezbollah commander, Israel alleges
events.description: Ibrahim Muhammad Ghazali, brother of the Michigan synagogue attacker, was a Hezbollah commander killed in an Israeli strike, Israeli intelligence revealed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Brother of Michigan synagogue attacker was Hezbollah commander, Israel alleges

events.title: Severe flooding kills 66 in Kenya, as heavy rains continue
events.description: Eleven people were rescued overnight after a minibus taxi got stuck in rising floodwaters in Nairobi.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Severe flooding kills 66 in Kenya, as heavy rains continue

events.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
events.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What Iranians are being told about the war

events.title: Cory Booker calls both parties ‘feckless’ for ceding war powers to Trump
events.description: Democrat says Congress ‘doing nothing’ may embolden president to attack countries such as Cuba and North Korea Democratic US senator Cory Booker has criticized both his own political party as well as its Republican counterpart for being “feckless” in ceding congressional war powers to Donald Trump , saying that their decision could embolden the president to unilaterally attack Cuba, North Korea and other countries. “I’m going to be one of those Democrats [who] say I think both parties have been feckless in allowing the growth of the power of the presidency,” Booker said on Sunday on CNN’s State of the Union. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Cory Booker calls both parties ‘feckless’ for ceding war powers to Trump

events.title: Apple cuts China App Store commission fees after government pressure
events.description: The move, which lowers fees to 25%, is a breakthrough for Chinese developers Tencent and ByteDance Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Apple announced late on Thursday it would lower the commission fees collected in its App Store in mainland China . The move follows pressure from regulators in the tech company’s second-largest market, as well as global scrutiny of its payment requirements. Fees for in-app purchases and paid transactions will be lowered to 25% from 30% starting on Sunday, Apple said in a statement on its blog for developers. Continue reading...

events.title: More than 60 dead after severe flooding in Kenya
events.description: A total of 62 people, including eight children, have died after floods hit Kenya last week.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ More than 60 dead after severe flooding in Kenya

events.title: With Middle East ‘pushed to breaking point’, UN chief urges ceasefire, return to diplomacy
events.description: The UN chief on Thursday urged all combatants to cease hostilities in the Middle East, uphold international law, protect civilians and make an immediate return to the negotiating table.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 With Middle East ‘pushed to breaking point’, UN chief urges ceasefire, return to diplomacy

events.title: State Department urges Americans to leave Middle East as airspace closures disrupt travel
events.description: Americans traveling in Dubai and Israel recount missile alerts, canceled flights and sheltering during regional conflict as the State Department urges citizens to leave.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 State Department urges Americans to leave Middle East as airspace closures disrupt travel

events.title: Why North Korea just fired rockets during US-South Korea military drills
events.description: North Korea launched multiple rockets toward a target 360 km away while Kim Jong Un observed with his daughter. The test coincides with joint US–South Korea military exercises and rising concerns about regional security. Story by Camille Corcoran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why North Korea just fired rockets during US-South Korea military drills

events.title: After attacks on Iran's oil facilities, toxic black rain endangers the public
events.description: Black rain fell in Iran's capital after airstrikes on oil facilities sent up dark clouds of toxic smoke
events.ticker_label: 🪖 After attacks on Iran's oil facilities, toxic black rain endangers the public

events.title: North Korean leader Kim observes test of rocket launch systems with his daughter
events.description: North Korea says leader Kim Jong Un has observed a live-fire test of multiple rocket launch systems to examine the country’s war deterrence
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korean leader Kim observes test of rocket launch systems with his daughter

events.title: UN chief launches major humanitarian appeal from war-torn Lebanon
events.description: In a solidarity visit to the war-ravaged capital of Lebanon, the UN chief on Friday announced a flash humanitarian appeal of $308.3 million to support civilians there, after ongoing Israeli strikes countering rocket launches from Hezbollah left around 816,000 Lebanese internally displaced, a figure that looks set to rise.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN chief launches major humanitarian appeal from war-torn Lebanon

events.title: World Economic Forum faces fresh scrutiny as Epstein ties revive past scandals, criticism
events.description: The World Economic Forum faces renewed scrutiny after Epstein links surfaced, adding to years of controversy surrounding Davos, leadership scandals and criticism of the globalist agenda.

events.title: How did Oscar winner Kpop Demon Hunters win hearts the world over?
events.description: We find out how the award-winning, demon-busting crooners have cast a spell world over.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How did Oscar winner Kpop Demon Hunters win hearts the world over?

events.title: Israeli troops kill 4 in Palestinian family in West Bank
events.description: A Palestinian family of four has been killed after Israeli forces opened fire on a vehicle in the occupied West Bank. In Gaza, a family of three and a teenage neighbor were killed in an apparent Israeli strike.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli troops kill 4 in Palestinian family in West Bank

events.title: World News in Brief: Attacks on synagogues, Syria’s continuing rights violations, shocking abuse of women during childbirth
events.description: The first two weeks of March have seen a surge in violent attacks and acts of intimidation targeting synagogues and Jewish institutions across North America and Europe.

events.title: Middle East war: Women in Lebanon forced to give birth on roadside
events.description: As the UN Secretary-General touched down in Beirut on Friday in solidarity with the people of Lebanon, UN agencies highlighted the dangers for civilians and particularly pregnant women and migrant workers, amid ongoing airstrikes and rocket fire between Hezbollah fighters and Israel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: Women in Lebanon forced to give birth on roadside

events.title: Rafah crossing closure leaves Gaza patients trapped without treatment
events.description: Thousands face worsening illness after closure of key crossing halts medical evacuations.

events.title: Israeli attacks kill 13 in Gaza, including 2 children and a pregnant woman
events.description: Meanwhile, more than 20,000 patients await evacuation as the Rafah crossing is set to partially reopen on Wednesday.

events.title: Ukraine eyes money and tech in return for Middle East drone support
events.description: Ukraine leader previously said advisers were sent to Qatar, UAE, and Saudi Arabia to help thwart Iranian drone attacks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine eyes money and tech in return for Middle East drone support

events.title: ‘Invasive’ AI-led mass surveillance in Africa violating freedoms, warn experts
events.description: Countries across the continent have spent more than $2bn on Chinese tracking technology that is not ‘necessary or proportionate’, new report finds The rapid expansion of AI-powered mass-surveillance systems across Africa is violating citizens’ right to privacy and having a chilling effect on society, according to experts on human rights and emerging technologies. At least $2bn (£1.5bn) has been spent by 11 African governments on Chinese-built surveillance technology that recognises faces and monitors movements, according to a new report by the Institute of Development Studies, which warns that national security is being used to justify implementing these systems with little regulation. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Invasive’ AI-led mass surveillance in Africa violating freedoms, warn experts

events.title: An Iranian-American writer describes the "vertigo" he feels about the war
events.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with Iranian American writer Nick Mafi about the war in Iran. Mafi says many Iranians in the US are feeling a sense of vertigo because of the conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 An Iranian-American writer describes the "vertigo" he feels about the war

events.title: Iran war: Tehran sees no reason to talk with US
events.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi rejected Trump's claim that Tehran wants a deal to end the war. Meanwhile, Germany said it will not play an "active part" in the conflict. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Tehran sees no reason to talk with US

events.title: UN relief chief condemns '$1 billion-a-day' cost of war in Middle East
events.description: The UN’s emergency relief chief on Wednesday condemned the “$1 billion-a-day” cost of the war in the Middle East, at a time when humanitarian needs are soaring and aid funding is falling dangerously short.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN relief chief condemns '$1 billion-a-day' cost of war in Middle East

events.title: High school senior charged with 'random' murder after 68-year-old woman found dead in Massachusetts home
events.description: Massachusetts teenager Anthony DeMayo, 18, was charged with murder in an alleged random attack that killed Janet Swallow, 68, in her Danvers home Thursday.

events.title: US offers $10M reward for info on Iran’s new supreme leader, top IRGC officials
events.description: The U.S. is offering a $10 million reward for intel on Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and IRGC officials accused of attacks against Americans.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US offers $10M reward for info on Iran’s new supreme leader, top IRGC officials

events.title: Iran deploys explosive ‘suicide skiffs’ disguised as fishing boats in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iranian suicide skiffs threaten global shipping as Supreme Leader vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed, prompting U.S. Navy escort discussions and surging oil price

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Five arrested in Cuba after protest at local Communist party office
events.description: Rare action began peacefully but ‘degenerated into vandalism’ according to state-run newspaper Five people have been arrested in Cuba for acts of “vandalism” after a small group of protesters broke into a provincial office of the Cuban Communist party and set fire to computers and furniture. The incident, which also affected a pharmacy and another shop, took place in the town of Moron, a little more than 300 miles (500km) east of Havana. Continue reading...

events.title: A young girl is knocked over at Tokyo crossing – what’s behind Japan’s ‘bumping’ trend?
events.description: Viral video of girl being shoved by fellow pedestrian has reignited debate over butsukari – with experts blaming stress and gender dynamics It starts out as a heartwarming clip. A young girl, clearly delighted to be in Tokyo, beams as she makes a peace sign to the camera. Seconds later, she is shoved to the ground from behind by a woman wearing a surgical mask. The assailant doesn’t skip a beat, striding out of shot of the clip filmed by the girl’s mother. This was no accidental clash of shoulders in a crowded place, but one of the most visible examples of a spate of butsukari otoko – “bumping man” – shoving incidents in Japan that experts attribute to a combination of gender dynamics and the stresses of modern life. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A young girl is knocked over at Tokyo crossing – what’s behind Japan’s ‘bumping’ trend?

events.title: Ukraine's urgent fight on the financial frontline
events.description: The war-torn country is battling to secure crucial funding from the IMF and EU, as well as putting up taxes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine's urgent fight on the financial frontline

events.title: In a small Lebanese town, grief and fear follow the Michigan synagogue attack
events.description: The suspect who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon on March 5. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 In a small Lebanese town, grief and fear follow the Michigan synagogue attack

events.title: Madagascar anti-corruption chief appointed as new PM
events.description: Madagascar's strongman leader has appointed Mamitiana Rajaonarison as PM days after dismissing the last government. Rajaonarison previously led the country's financial intelligence and money laundering watchdog.

events.title: Middle East war live: Flights suspended at Dubai airport after drone sparks nearby fire
events.description: Flights were temporarily suspended at Dubai's airport on Monday after a "drone-related incident" sparked a fire nearby, city authorities said. The Emirati defence ministry later said its air defence systems were intercepting a wave of Iranian drones and missiles, without specifying where. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.

events.title: Being an artist and a refugee: Amir Amiri and Parisa Karimi Molan speak to France 24

events.title: Socialist leads Paris mayoral race but leftist rival threatens to split vote

But in the capital as in other cities across France, a strong showing by Jean-Luc Mélenchon's France Unbowed (LFI) could complicate matters for the mainstream left.

events.title: Eswatini says it received more ‘third country’ deportees as part of deal with Trump administration
events.description: Two deportees sent to Eswatini were from Somalia, one was from Sudan and another was from Tanzania The government of Eswatini announced on Thursday it received four more “third country” deportees from the United States, as part of the Trump administration ’s multimillion-dollar deal with the small African nation. Now a total of 19 deportees from the US have been sent to Eswatini even as they hail from other countries, amid the Trump administration’s continued anti-immigrant crackdown and changes to immigration policy. Continue reading...

events.title: China’s rubber-stamp parliament set to approve ‘ethnic unity’ law
events.description: New legislation will require schools to use Mandarin by default, taking priority over minority ethnic languages such as Tibetan, Uyghur and Mongolian China’s National People’s Congress (NPC), the state legislature, will vote on Thursday on a suite of new laws agreed at this year’s annual two sessions gathering , including a piece of legislation that will diminish the role of minority ethnic languages in the education system. NPC delegates are expected to approve a new ethnic unity law, along with a new environmental code and the 15th five-year plan, the economic planning document for 2026-2030. Delegates have spent the last week debating Beijing’s proposed bills, which they are all but certain to approve. The NPC, which is often described as a rubber-stamp parliament, has never rejected an item on its agenda. Continue reading...

events.title: Virgin Australia flight met by firefighters at Melbourne airport after smoke seen coming from vape
events.description: Passenger on Flight VA 328 alerted the crew who responded by ‘containing the device’ before plane landed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Virgin Australia flight from Brisbane to Melbourne was met by firefighters after smoke was seen coming from a vape on board. Flight VA 328 landed safely and all passengers disembarked normally after a vape “activated” in the cabin shortly before landing at about 4pm on Sunday afternoon in Melbourne, Melbourne airport said. Continue reading...

events.title: France returns sacred talking drum looted from Côte d’Ivoire over 100 years ago
events.description: Djidji Ayôkwé was handed to Ivorian officials in Paris earlier this month A sacred artefact looted by French colonial authorities more than a century ago has been returned to Côte d’Ivoire in one of the most significant cultural restitutions to a former French colony in years. The Djidji Ayôkwé , a talking drum confiscated in 1916 by French administrators, landed at 8.45am on Friday at the airport in Port Bouët on the outskirts of the economic capital, Abidjan. It was handed over to Ivorian officials in Paris earlier this month after being removed from the Quai Branly – Jacques Chirac Museum. Continue reading...

events.title: ACM to brief staff after co-owner Antony Catalano charged with assault of a woman
events.description: Australian Community Media says employee ‘wellbeing’ top priority amid ‘shock and deep concern’ over 59-year-old’s charges Australian Community Media will hold a staff meeting on Monday afternoon to share the regional media group’s response to the arrest of its part-owner Antony Catalano who is facing charges of assault of a woman. On Sunday the board and executive leadership team of ACM distanced itself from the 59-year-old newspaper mogul, saying they were “shocked and deeply concerned” about the charges. Continue reading...

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Nigeria’s online content creator market has boomed. Can the skit-makers and streamers make it pay?
events.description: As platforms make less from advertising, creators are struggling to monetise work – leading to calls for more government investment and tax breaks On a humid afternoon in Lagos, a shoot for a comedy skit is under way on a set that looks more like a small film production. Dozens of people mill about: lighting assistants, a sound engineer, a makeup artist and even a content creator recording unscripted behind-the-scenes footage. At the centre is Broda Shaggi, born Samuel Animashaun Perry, who is issuing instructions, rehearsing lines and performing caricatures. Continue reading...

events.title: Author Craig Silvey facing more charges after being accused of producing child exploitation material
events.description: Jasper Jones author also charged with additional count of possessing child exploitation material Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian author Craig Silvey has been charged with more child exploitation offences. The 43-year-old was previously charged with possessing and distributing child exploitation material in early January. Continue reading...

events.title: Nine-year-old Australian boy allegedly caught driving car at 1.30am on major highway
events.description: NSW police allege child was sitting on lap of 45-year-old man who was experiencing ‘significant fatigue’ but was operating pedals while boy steered Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A nine-year-old boy has allegedly been caught steering a car after 1am on a major highway in New South Wales. NSW police alleged that a 45-year-old driver was experiencing “significant fatigue” and “operating the pedals” while the boy steered on his lap. Continue reading...

events.title: 5th member of Iranian women's soccer team gives up asylum in Australia
events.description: The player's departure shortly before midnight on Sunday leaves two of an initial seven squad members in Australia.

events.title: Brazil's ex-President Bolsonaro is in intensive care with pneumonia, hospital says
events.description: One of Bolsonaro's doctors described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."

events.title: Republic of Congo votes in presidential election
events.description: President Dennis Sassou N'Guesso, 82, seems set to win a fifth term in a row in Sunday's election in the Republic of Congo. Sassou N'Guesso is already one of Africa's longest-serving leaders.

events.title: Vietnam: Polls open in one-party election
events.description: The Communist Party has fielded nearly 93% of the total candidates in the election.

events.title: Ligue 1: Lyon can't beat Le Havre and see OM take off in race for 3rd
events.description: Olympique Lyonnais could only manage a 0-0 draw on the road against Le Havre. Lyon (4th) has fallen behind Marseille (3rd) and is seeing Lille (5th) close in.

events.title: 🔴 'One Battle After Another' wins Oscar for best picture
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Nicolas Sarkozy back in court over alleged Libyan funding for 2007 presidential campaign
events.description: Former French president Nicolas Sarkozy returns to court on Monday to challenge his conviction over allegations he sought campaign funding from Muammar Gaddafi’s Libya for his 2007 election. Sarkozy, who denies wrongdoing, briefly served time last year. The Paris appeals court retrial will run until June 3.

events.title: Left-wing candidate Emmanuel Grégoire tops first round of Paris mayoral race, exit polls say
events.description: No description available.

events.title: How UK might try to keep the Strait of Hormuz open
events.description: The UK military is considering the deployment of two drone types to the Middle East after the US asked allies for help to secure a key shipping lane in the region, it is understood.

events.title: Cuban political activist says police not allowing her to leave home
events.description: Yoani Sánchez says a purported policeman in civilian clothes is stopping her.

events.title: Brazil ex-President Bolsonaro's kidney function improves but he remains in intensive care
events.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro’s kidney function has improved but he will remain in an intensive care unit because of pneumonia

events.title: Pakistan says it hit militant hideouts in Afghanistan’s Kandahar as fighting shows no letup
events.description: Pakistan says it struck militant hideouts in Afghanistan as cross-border fighting intensified and both sides traded blame
events.ticker_label: 💥 Pakistan says it hit militant hideouts in Afghanistan’s Kandahar as fighting shows no letup

events.title: Swedish court orders detention of Russian captain of tanker boarded off Sweden
events.description: A Swedish court has ordered the detention of the Russian captain of a ship which was suspected to be sailing under a false flag in the Baltic Sea and was boarded by authorities last week

events.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine is waiting on US, Russia to set next round of talks
events.description: Ukrainian president says he is ready for the next trilateral peace talks.

events.title: Orbán and challenger Magyar summon rival rallies in show of strength before Hungary's April election
events.description: Hungary’s election fight is intensifying as Viktor Orbán and challenger Péter Magyar stage rival mass rallies in Budapest to show strength

events.title: French voters head to polls in early municipal test before 2027 presidential election
events.description: French voters are heading to the polls to choose new municipal teams in an early test for political parties before next year’s presidential elections

events.title: Kazakhs vote in referendum on new constitution that could keep the president in power
events.description: Kazakhs are voting Sunday in a referendum on a new constitution that analysts say could allow President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev to extend his hold on power beyond his current term

events.title: A bus carrying Indian pilgrims plunges off a mountain road in Nepal, killing 7
events.description: A bus carrying Indian pilgrims has slipped off a mountain road in central Nepal, killing seven people and injuring nine others

events.title: A blow to Caribbean democracy as Stabroek News and Newsday papers fold after social media shift
events.description: Two major Caribbean newspapers are shutting down as readers shift to social media and ad money dries up

events.title: Former substitute teacher and boyfriend face 38 child sex charges as bond nears 9 million
events.description: A Texas substitute teacher and her boyfriend, accused of 38 child sex crimes, see bonds jump to nearly $9 million as new charges filed in shocking abuse case.

events.title: Headless, handless body found on New York road 56 years ago identified through DNA; killer remains unknown
events.description: A DNA breakthrough identified a headless body found in New York 56 years ago as Clyde Coppage from Pennsylvania, but his killer still remains at large.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Headless, handless body found on New York road 56 years ago identified through DNA; killer remains unknown

events.title: Teens accused of plotting twisted 'blood ritual' school killing giggle in cruiser about glam shot
events.description: Lois Lippert and Isabelle Valdez were charged as adults after allegedly plotting a “blood ritual" killing at their Florida high school before another student tipped off authorities.

events.title: Former Gettysburg mayor arrested on child sex abuse charges weeks after resignation
events.description: Former Gettysburg Mayor Chad Alan-Carr faces child sex charges after resigning just two weeks into his term, allegedly grooming a teenage boy through theater connections.

events.title: Texas judge who played victim card booted from rodeo grounds a second time following VIP seating dispute
events.description: Harris County Judge Lina Hidalgo is feuding with the Houston Rodeo board after a VIP seating dispute at NRG Stadium after she was escorted out of the venue.

events.title: American flag raised, flies over US Embassy building in Venezuela for first time in 7 years
events.description: The U.S. Embassy in Caracas raised the American flag for the first time in seven years, months after former President Nicolás Maduro's capture by U.S. forces.

events.title: Transgender triple killer removed from home with 2 foster children months after authorities were notified
events.description: Australian authorities apologize after two foster children lived with a transgender convicted triple killer for months, calling the situation "entirely unacceptable" and "terrible."
events.ticker_label: 💥 Transgender triple killer removed from home with 2 foster children months after authorities were notified
